what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record for the design-source channel; it declares an image attachment with a unique id, a target URI pointing to an external image asset, and a timestamp indicating when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is a URI of the form image://design-source/company-info/p-13.
It includes an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone in the 'added' field.
The file name and id share the same att-da4a6510 identifier.
standouts: References an external image asset via a custom image:// URI scheme.
Serves as a typed, timestamped link between a channel and an image.
Contains no descriptive text or caption about the image itself.
conventions: Names the attachment file with an att-<hash>.md pattern.
Records channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses a URI-style target rather than a relative file path.
Stores timestamps in ISO 8601 with explicit timezone offset.
concerns: The target URI cannot be resolved or validated from within this file alone.
There is no caption, title, or description of the attached image.
No author or provenance information is recorded.
The opaque hash in the id provides no semantic hint about the image content.
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata stub that links the design-source channel to an external image asset via a custom URI.
questions: What visual content does image://design-source/company-info/p-13 represent?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual storage location?
Who added this attachment and for what design decision?
Are attachments expected to be immutable once added?
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a lightweight, versionable ledger entry that associates a design channel with an image asset without embedding the image itself.
novelty: It uses a custom image:// URI scheme to address a design image asset rather than a conventional file path or URL.
contribution: It preserves a timestamped, typed association between the design-source channel and image asset p-13, giving the attachment an identity and a stable reference independent of the image file's storage location.
summary_for_embedding: This is a channel attachment metadata file that records an image attachment (att-da4a6510) in the design-source channel pointing to image://design-source/company-info/p-13 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and link an external image asset to the design-source channel as a persistent, timestamped reference.
